topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is a collection of various materials and tools used by System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: Who is the Book of Secret Knowledge repository aimed at?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers, but it can be useful for anyone.

entries.q: What type of content can be found in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository contains a collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: How can users contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can contribute to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by making pull requests and adding valid and well-reasoned explanations about their changes or comments.

entries.q: What is the license of the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge repository is licensed under the MIT License.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI tools listed in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some examples of CLI tools listed in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository include GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, and Oh My ZSH!

entries.q: What are some examples of shell plugins listed in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Some examples of shell plugins listed in the Book of Secret Knowledge repository include z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, and zsh-syntax-highlighting.

entries.q: How can users stay updated with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository?
entries.a: Users can stay updated with changes to the Book of Secret Knowledge repository by subscribing to the RSS/Atom feed of the commits.
